hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.subtitle: How we collect, use, and protect your information when you interact with BethGold Foundation online.

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
paragraphs: BethGold Foundation ("we," "us," "our," or "the Foundation") is committed to protecting the privacy of everyone who visits our website or interacts with us through it. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, why we collect it, how we use and protect it, and what rights you have regarding your information.

By using this website or submitting information through any of our forms, you agree to the practices described in this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree with this policy, please do not submit personal information through this site.

sections.heading: 2. Who We Are
paragraphs: BethGold Foundation is a registered NGO dedicated to empowering communities through education, health initiatives, and community development. This website (the "Site") is operated by BethGold Foundation.
If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your information, you can reach us using the contact details at the end of this policy.

sections.heading: 3. Scope of This Policy
paragraphs: BethGold Foundation is established and primarily operates in The Gambia, and this Privacy Policy is written to comply with the Data Protection Act, 2023 of The Gambia.

Because we welcome visitors, donors, volunteers, and partners from around the world, we also extend the data protection rights described in the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) to visitors from the European Union, European Economic Area, and the United Kingdom, regardless of whether Gambian law would otherwise require it. Where this policy refers to your rights, both frameworks are addressed separately in Section 9 below.

sections.heading: 4. Information We Collect
paragraphs: We only collect personal information that you choose to provide to us directly through the forms on this Site. We do not collect personal information automatically, and we do not currently use cookies, analytics, or tracking technologies of any kind (see Section 7, "Cookies and Analytics").
Depending on which form you use, we collect:
list: Contact form: your full name, email address, and the message you send us.
Volunteer application form: your full name, email address, your selected area of interest, your availability, and any additional information you choose to share about yourself.
Partnership inquiry form: your organization's name, your full name, your email address, and your message describing the partnership you have in mind.
closingParagraphs: We do not ask for, and you should not submit, sensitive personal information (such as national ID numbers, financial account details, or health information) through any form on this Site.

sections.heading: 5. How We Use Your Information
paragraphs: We use the information you submit only for the purpose you submitted it for:
list: To respond to your message or inquiry
To process and follow up on volunteer applications
To evaluate and respond to partnership proposals
To keep a record of our communications with you
closingParagraphs: We do not use your information for advertising, and we do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information to any third party.

sections.heading: 6. How We Share Your Information
paragraphs: Submissions from every form on this Site are processed through a trusted third-party form-processing service (currently Web3Forms), which securely delivers your submission to our email inbox. We do not control this provider's own data-handling practices beyond what is necessary to deliver your message to us, and we encourage you to review their privacy policy if you would like more detail.
We may update the form-processing service we use as the Site evolves; this policy will be updated to reflect any change.
Aside from the form-processing service above, we do not share your personal information with any other third party, except where required by law.

sections.heading: 7. Cookies and Analytics
paragraphs: This Site does not currently use cookies, web beacons, or any analytics or tracking technology. If we introduce any such tools in the future — for example, to understand how visitors use the Site — we will update this Privacy Policy accordingly and, where required by law, request your consent first.

sections.heading: 8. Data Retention
paragraphs: We retain the information you submit for as long as reasonably necessary to respond to your inquiry, maintain a record of volunteer applications and partnership discussions, and comply with our legal obligations. If you would like us to delete information you have submitted, contact us using the details in Section 13 below.

sections.heading: 9. Your Rights
paragraphs: Under The Gambia's Data Protection Act, 2023, you have the right to:
list: Be informed about how your personal information is collected and used
Access the personal information we hold about you
Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete information
Request deletion of your personal information
Object to or restrict certain uses of your information
closingParagraphs: If you are visiting from the European Union, European Economic Area, or United Kingdom, we additionally extend to you the rights recognized under the GDPR, including the right to data portability and the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority.
To exercise any of these rights, contact us using the details in Section 13 below. We will respond within a reasonable time.

sections.heading: 10. Children's Privacy
paragraphs: This Site is not directed at children, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children under the age of 16. If you believe a child has provided us with personal information, please contact us so we can delete it.

sections.heading: 11. Data Security
paragraphs: We take reasonable measures to protect the information submitted to us, including relying on our form-processing provider's security practices for data in transit. However, no method of electronic transmission or storage is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

sections.heading: 12. Changes to This Policy
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, the services we use, or legal requirements. The "Last Updated" date at the top of this page reflects the most recent revision. We encourage you to review this page periodically.

sections.heading: 13. Contact Us
paragraphs: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or your personal information, please contact us. We will be pleased to help.
